LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRUCE WILLIAMS' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ANNA SCOTT IS DENIED. MS. SCOTT ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT MR. WILLIAMS ACTED MALICIOUSLY AND OPPRESSIVELY (I.E., DESPICABLY AND WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE RIGHTS OF MS. SCOTT AND OTHERS) BY KNOWINGLY DRIVING A VEHICLE UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF ALCOHOL AND FLEEING THE SCENE OF THE ACCIDENT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

ASSIGN TO ARBITRATION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Select Panel Of Arbitrators

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Assigned To Arbitration On Oct-30-17

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANT BRUCE WILLIAMS' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Anna Scott is DENIED. Ms. Scott adequately alleges that Mr. Williams acted maliciously and oppressively (i.e., despicably and with a conscious disregard for the rights of Ms. Scott and others) by knowingly driving a vehicle under the influence of alcohol and fleeing the scene of the accident. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive Damages And Exemplary Damages In The First Amended Complaint, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof [Ccp Sections 435-437];
Off Calendar Jul-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRUCE WILLIAMS' Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive Damages And Exemplary Damages In The First Amended Complaint is Off calendar. Amended complaint filed. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2017 Continued To Aug-30-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. IAN BOOTH KELLEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-551-0889 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PALOMA L. PICASSO OF THE WASSON & ASSOCIATES, INC., TEL. NO. 1-714-368-0000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court vacated its tentative ruling and ruled a follows: Defendant Bruce Williams' motion to strike punitive damages allegations from the complaint is granted with leave to amend. No opposition filed. The First Amended Complaint to be filed within 20 days. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(302/HEK)
